summary: High‑octane NYE set with epic The Other One sequence & tight jams
blurb: The December 31, 1971 Winterland show roars through a monster The Other One → Me & My Uncle → The Other One suite and a classic Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road > Not Fade Away sandwich. Jerry’s lightning‑bolt leads, Keith’s fresh piano fills, and Pigpen’s wild energy keep the momentum rolling from start to finish.
review:
On New Year’s Eve 1971 the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying Winterland outings. Opening with a punchy “Dancin’ In The Street” they built a relentless first set that culminated in Jerry Garcia’s scorching solo on “Next Time You See Me” and a rollicking “Playing In The Band.” The second set launched into the legendary “Truckin' → Drums → The Other One → Me & My Uncle → The Other One” marathon, where Jerry threw lightning‑bolt leads and Keith Godchaux added bright piano fills that helped define the new 1971 sound. A seamless Not Fade Away > Goin’ Down the Road Feeling Bad > Not Fade Away sandwich kept the groove hot, while “Big River” featured an unexpected but powerful vocal from Garcia. The night closed with a smoking “Casey Jones,” leaving fans ringing in 1972 on a high‑energy wave that still feels fresh today.

Across the multiple sources—soundboard captures, cleaned FM broadcasts and new transfers—the performance is uniformly praised: Jerry’s guitar is described as “on fire” and “soaring,” Keith’s piano is noted for depth and space‑filled fills, Pigpen’s harmonica and on‑stage humor add character, and Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is called “furious” and “killing the kit.” The show’s setlist flow, tight transitions, and historic moments (Keith’s debut, Donna Jean’s first appearance) make this NYE concert a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s incendiary leads, the epic The Other One suite, Keith’s fresh piano work, and Bill’s high‑energy drumming. Consistent acclaim across sources warrants a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent new transfer with perfect speed/pitch and crystal‑clear clarity makes it the most listenable version

key_highlights: Epic The Other One → Me & My Uncle → The Other One suite with soaring leads
Classic Not Fade Away > Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad > Not Fade Away sandwich
Jerry’s surprising vocal on Big River and scorching solo on Next Time You See Me
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers lightning‑bolt guitar work, scorching solo on Next Time You See Me and a powerful vocal on Big River; his leads on The Other One suite are described as soaring and on fire.
band_performance.Phil: Provides crisp, driving bass throughout, especially tight in the early set and underpinning the China Cat Sunflower transition.
band_performance.Bob: Adds wild Pigpen harmonica energy, humor (the diaper anecdote), and charismatic vocals on numbers like The Same Thing; his NYE story adds narrative flair.
band_performance.Billy: Furious, rolling drumming that ‘kills the kit,’ driving the high‑octane NFA sandwich, Truckin', and the entire second set with relentless power.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
